item.title: Ontario drivers travelling solo could soon use HOV lanes, province says
Canada is copying Europe’s TikTok curbs, but without the teeth
item.description: The post Canada is copying Europe’s TikTok curbs, but without the teeth appeared first on The Logic .

item.title: Toronto in for a windy and overcast Tuesday with frigid wind chill
Two men in hospital following stabbing at Bloor-Yonge subway station Monday night
Doug Ford defends FOI changes, warns of enemies seeking government secrets
item.description: Ford said he's tightening the laws to "protect" himself and his cabinet ministers from "communist China" and other hostile powers.

item.title: Doug Ford has floated the idea of building a Toronto convention centre on Lake Ontario. Here’s what it would take
Toronto police set to release OPP report on Umar Zameer case, officer perjury allegations
Israel seeking ‘significant change’ in how Canada tackles antisemitism
item.description: OTTAWA - Israel is pursuing a sweeping diplomatic and public relations campaign to convince Canada to change the way it tackles acts of antisemitism.

item.title: Lawful access bill could create vulnerabilities for hackers, experts warn
item.description: Foreign intelligence services with malevolent intent could also exploit intercept infrastructure, tech and legal specialists say

item.title: Longest Ballot Committee collecting signatures for potential candidates in Terrebonne by-election
item.description: Group aims to load up ballots with dozens of little-known candidates as a way to protest electoral system

item.title: A Toronto councillor wants to create public grocery stores to tackle rising food costs. Would that work here?
item.description: The pilot would have four city-run grocery stores, prioritizing lower-income households and neighbourhoods with limited access to grocery stores.

item.title: Politics Insider: Carney in London for high-profile meetings
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister met with U.K.’s Starmer, discussed war in Iran

item.title: Doug Ford wants to livestream bail hearings. Experts say it would jeopardize the court process: ‘I don’t think that it serves justice’
Carney meets Keir Starmer in London as war in Iran disrupts shipping routes
item.description: The leaders discussed the "situation in the Middle East" and condemned Iran's missile and drone attacks on civilians and energy infrastructure…

item.title: Ford government says public safety concerns behind decision to drop funding for supervised consumption sites
Premier Ford urges harsh handling of Project South accused charged with conspiring to kill a jail official
NDP leadership hopeful rebuffed opponent’s offer of second-ballot support
Mark Carney joins call for Israel not to invade Lebanon
Canada can ‘manage’ Trump’s call for help without being drawn into Iran war: minister
Doug Ford says he wants to livestream bail hearings as he announces new jail
item.description: Ontario Premier Doug Ford said Monday his government is exploring the idea of live-streaming bail hearings to the public.

item.title: ‘We got to protect ourselves:’ Doug Ford cites threat from China in defending cabinet secrecy
item.description: Ontario Premier Doug Ford is defending his government’s decision to exclude his office and those of his cabinet ministers from Freedom of Information (FOI) laws, saying the move is necessary to protect the privacy of his constituents and to safeguard information from foreign entities.

item.title: Doug Ford says his cellphone records must remain hidden to protect privacy
item.description: Last week his government announced that the Premier, cabinet ministers and their offices would no longer be subject to freedom of information requests

item.title: Ford accused of limiting transparency law because of cellphone defeat in court
item.description: Ontario Premier Doug Ford saw his attempts to stop his cellphone records being partially released blocked by a panel of judges. Critics say he's changing the law as a result.

item.title: Nature groups warn federal funding cuts endangering efforts to protect land, water
Ottawa puts $200M into space launch pad in Nova Scotia
item.description: Ottawa will use the funds over the next decade to lease a space launch pad near Canso, Nova Scotia, which will serve as the foundation for a future spaceport.

item.title: Doug Ford is changing a law to shield politicians from scrutiny
Why Canada is helping Canadians flee the Middle East — and what it costs
Carney government in talks with Saskatchewan to fast-track energy projects
Doug Ford wants jets at Billy Bishop. Here’s what is at stake for Toronto’s waterfront — and why some say there’s a ‘missing ingredient’
Doug Ford said ‘whisper jets’ were coming to Billy Bishop. Here’s what aviation experts say about airplane noise
item.description: Ontario's premier said the jets coming to Billy Bishop airport would be quieter than the current turboprop planes.

item.title: Canada's annual inflation rate fell to 1.8% in February, war's impact not yet reflected
item.description: Canada's annual inflation rate fell to 1.8 per cent in February, Statistics Canada said on Monday.

item.title: Carney, Starmer meet amid deepening Middle East crisis
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney and U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer met in London to discuss the escalating Middle East war and the closure of the Strait of Hormuz. The leaders condemned Iran's attacks, warned of mounting civilian and economic costs, and stressed the need to reopen the critical oil shipping route.

item.title: Canada joins allies in warning against Israeli ground offensive in Lebanon
item.description: "The humanitarian situation ⁠in Lebanon, including ongoing mass displacement, is already deeply alarming," said a statement by ​Canada, France, Britain, Germany and Italy.

item.title: ‘One elbow up, one down’: The Carney government after its first year
Poilievre arrives late to the auto debate with a plan from 1965
item.description: It’s as if the Conservative Leader can’t believe the happy days are gone and thinks a simple plan will bring them back

item.title: Ontario urges federal government to legalize pepper spray for self-defence
item.description: Ontario is calling on the federal government to legally allow people to carry and use pepper spray for self-defence.

item.title: Poilievre pitches tariff-free auto industry pact with U.S.
item.description: Conservative Leader says Canada can’t steer away from its biggest export market if it wants to revive sector

item.title: Company offers use of private deep-water Baffin Island port to Department of National Defence
item.description: Defence Minister was non-committal when asked about the possibility of the military using Milne Port

item.title: Doug Ford to seek an injunction to block Al-Quds Day rally in Toronto
item.description: The premier's move comes in the wake of a similar move by two city councillors.

item.title: Canadian competitive sports shooters caught in the crosshairs of sweeping federal gun control reforms
Poilievre unveils auto plan aiming for tariff-free access to U.S. market
item.description: Conservative Leader Pierre Poilievre unveiled the party's most substantial auto strategy to date under his leadership — one he says will both bring back production to Canada and be "highly attractive" to partners south of the border.

item.title: Canada, Nordic leaders aim to deepen ties as trade being used as ‘coercive tool’
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney is concluding his time in Norway meeting with that country’s prime minister, along with the leaders of Denmark, Iceland, Sweden and Finland.

item.title: Thousands attend pro-Palestinian rally in Toronto after judge denies Ford government’s injunction to stop it
Mark Carney helping push Liberals ahead of the Conservatives, poll suggests
Nordic leaders meet in Oslo with Carney as Arctic tensions sharpen over Greenland, Russia
item.description: Leaders from the five Nordic countries and Canada gathered in Oslo for a mini-summit on Arctic security amid rising geopolitical tensions. Discussions follow a massive NATO exercise in northern Norway and focus on Russian military risks, the fallout from U.S. threats to seize Greenland and energy security.

item.title: Carney says Canada a reliable oil exporter with moves to increase production
item.description: The prime minister's comments come as oil markets reel from the war in the Middle East, which has closed oil shipments through the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Prime Minister’s office declines to weigh in on Order of Canada petition for Don Cherry
item.description: Conservative MP gathering signatures for online petition to award Canada’s highest honorary distinction to hockey commentator

item.title: Carney defends his economic record after one year in office
item.description: Prime Minister is in Norway as part of ongoing efforts to open new trade and business opportunities

item.title: Canada’s Olympic medal letdown masks the real sports crisis in this country. One potential game changer is already on the table
Carney, Norway PM push message of oil-market stability as war in Middle East rattles global supply
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney and Norway’s Jonas Gahr Støre stressed energy stability during talks in Oslo as the Middle East war rattles global oil markets. Canada will release 23.6 million barrels following an International Energy Agency call for coordinated action. Both leaders also urged de-escalation and pledged closer Canada-Norway cooperation on Arctic security.

item.title: Michael Ford reveals why he may run for mayor of Toronto — and what he’s done since leaving Queen’s Park
item.description: The nephew of Premier Doug Ford says he has been consulting the public for months about a potential mayoral run.

item.title: Federal government planning to revamp funding for Canadian athletes, says Carney
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney says Ottawa is planning to revamp its funding for Canadian athletes and will examine the issue "very deliberately" over the next six months — news that comes just before a federal commission on the future of Canadian sport releases its final report.

item.title: New café offers taste of Quebec independence
item.description: When you walk into the Club Pays coffee shop in Montreal, the first thing you see are the posters with Quebec sovereignty slogans plastered on every wall.
